title: Manulea rubra
intro: Manulea rubra, also known as red manulea, is a flowering plant species in the family Scrophulariaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Manulea rubra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Manulea rubra is often made into teas, infusions, and decoctions to treat various ailments, and it is also used in the form of tinctures and capsules for its purported health benefits.
taxonomy: Manulea rubra is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, commonly known as the figwort family. This family is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Northern Hemisphere and are known for their distinctive flowers and leaves. Manulea rubra is a member of the genus Manulea, which is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in South Africa. The Scrophulariaceae family is closely related to other families such as the Orobanchaceae and the Plantaginaceae. Manulea rubra's classification is further supported by its shared characteristics with other members of the Scrophulariaceae family.
